meanings.definition: a United States term for Blacks that is now considered offensive
affect as in thought or feeling
examples: My personal feelings color my judgment in this case
His political ideas color his lectures
color the walls with paint in warm tones

meanings.definition: give a deceptive explanation or excuse for
change color, often in an undesired manner
having color or a certain color; sometimes used in combination
having skin rich in melanin pigments
examples: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People

meanings.definition: favoring one person or side over another
examples: a biased account of the trial
a decision that was partial to the defendant

meanings.definition: (used of color) artificially produced; not natural
